examples.title: File Descriptor Passing using Unix Domain Sockets
examples.description: Demonstrates passing a file descriptor from parent to child using socketpair and SCM_RIGHTS
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates passing a file descriptor from parent to child. Parent opens a file, creates a Unix domain socket pair, forks, then uses sendmsg() with SCM_RIGHTS to pass the FD. Child uses recvmsg() to extract the FD from control messages and can then read from the file.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for file I/O, O(1) for FD transfer

examples.title: SCM_RIGHTS Control Message Flow
examples.description: High-level pseudocode showing the control message setup for passing file descriptors
examples.code:
// Sender Process
PROCEDURE send_file_descriptor(socket_fd, file_descriptor)
 // Step 1: Allocate buffer for control message
 buffer_size = CMSG_SPACE(sizeof(int)) // Space for FD
 control_buffer = allocate(buffer_size)
 
 // Step 2: Setup msghdr structure
 msg = new msghdr
 msg.msg_iov = iov_buffer // Can include regular data
 msg.msg_iovlen = 1
 msg.msg_control = control_buffer
 msg.msg_controllen = buffer_size
 
 // Step 3: Setup cmsghdr for SCM_RIGHTS
 cmsg = CMSG_FIRSTHDR(msg)
 cmsg.cmsg_level = SOL_SOCKET
 cmsg.cmsg_type = SCM_RIGHTS
 cmsg.cmsg_len = CMSG_LEN(sizeof(int))
 
 // Step 4: Copy file descriptor into cmsg data
 fd_location = CMSG_DATA(cmsg)
 STORE fd_location = file_descriptor
 
 // Step 5: Send message with ancillary data
 result = sendmsg(socket_fd, msg, 0)
 
 // Step 6: Close original FD (optional but recommended)
 close(file_descriptor)

// Receiver Process
PROCEDURE receive_file_descriptor(socket_fd)
 // Step 1: Allocate buffer for control message
 buffer_size = CMSG_SPACE(sizeof(int))
 control_buffer = allocate(buffer_size)
 
 // Step 2: Setup msghdr structure
 msg = new msghdr
 msg.msg_iov = iov_buffer // For receiving data
 msg.msg_iovlen = 1
 msg.msg_control = control_buffer
 msg.msg_controllen = buffer_size
 
 // Step 3: Receive message
 result = recvmsg(socket_fd, msg, 0)
 
 // Step 4: Find SCM_RIGHTS in control messages
 FOR EACH cmsg IN msg.msg_control
 IF cmsg.cmsg_level == SOL_SOCKET AND cmsg.cmsg_type == SCM_RIGHTS THEN
 fd_ptr = CMSG_DATA(cmsg)
 received_fd = LOAD fd_ptr
 RETURN received_fd
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN -1 // No FD received
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the logical flow of sending and receiving file descriptors using control messages. Key steps involve setting up msghdr, allocating cmsghdr with SCM_RIGHTS type at SOL_SOCKET level, and using CMSG_DATA to access the actual file descriptor value.

examples.title: Privilege Separation Pattern with FD Passing
examples.description: Server pattern where privileged parent passes socket to unprivileged child
examples.explanation: This demonstrates privilege separation where a privileged parent process accepts network connections but delegates them to unprivileged child processes for actual handling. The parent passes the accepted socket to the child using file descriptor passing, minimizing the time the child runs with elevated privileges.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per connection for FD passing
